title: 3d printing automation assisted by integrated robotic system
abstract: Aspects relate to a three-dimensional printing device (100) including a gantry system configured to move a build platform between the different stages of the printing process. The stages may include, for example, printing, washing, and post curing. The gantry system (140) includes a gantry rail (144) and a gantry carriage (142). The gantry carriage can be removably attached to a build platform (102) on which a printed object (104) is printed and the gantry rail can then move the build platform between at least the printing and washing stages.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A three-dimensional (3D) printing device, comprising: a build platform; a printing module configured to print a printed object on the build platform, the printing module comprising a resin tank and a first linear actuator configured to move the build platform in a linear motion into and out of the resin tank; a washing module configured to rinse the printed object, the washing module comprising a washing solvent tank and a second linear actuator configured to move the printed object on the build platform in a linear motion into and out of the washing solvent tank; and a gantry system comprising a gantry rail and a gantry carriage, the gantry carriage configured to be removably attached to the build platform, the gantry rail configured to move the build platform comprising the printed object from the first linear actuator to the second linear actuator. 2. The 3D printing device of claim 1, further comprising: a post curing module configured to cure the printed object. 3. The 3D printing device of claim 2, wherein the post curing module comprises a curing tank and a third linear actuator configured to move the printed object in a linear motion into and out of the curing tank, wherein the gantry rail is further 

configured to move the build platform comprising the printed object from the second linear actuator to the third linear actuator. 4. The 3D printing device of claim 2, further comprising: an additional build platform, wherein the gantry system is configured to detach from the build platform and attach to the additional build platform to print an additional printed object on the additional build platform during washing or curing of the printed object. 5. The 3D printing device of claim 1, wherein the gantry carriage comprises a first set of magnetic tips and the build platform comprises a second set of magnetic tips, wherein the first set of magnetic tips are configured to magnetically attach to the second set of magnetic tips. 6. The 3D printing device of claim 1, further comprising: electromagnets configured to magnetically attach the build platform to each of the first linear actuator and the second linear actuator. 7. The 3D printing device of claim 6, wherein: the first linear actuator comprises a first electromagnet carriage configured to magnetically attach to the build platform to secure the build platform to the first linear actuator, wherein the gantry carriage is detached from the build platform upon attaching the build platform to the first linear actuator; and 

 the second linear actuator comprises a second electromagnet carriage configured to magnetically attach to the build platform to secure the build platform to the second linear actuator, wherein the gantry carriage is detached from the build platform upon attaching the build platform to the first linear actuator. 8. The 3D printing device of claim 7, wherein the build platform comprises grooves configured to facilitate magnetic attachment to the electromagnetic carriage. 9. The 3D printing device of claim 6, further comprising: magnetic field sensors configured to monitor a magnetic field strength of the electromagnets and to generate an alert in response to the magnetic field strength decreasing below a threshold. 10. The 3D printing device of claim 1, wherein the printing module further comprises: an ultraviolet light engine configured to project a pattern into the resin tank to form the printed object on the build platform; and an alignment tool configured to align the build platform on the first linear actuator with the ultraviolet light engine. 11. The 3D printing device of claim 10, wherein the alignment tool comprises: a laser configured to produce collimated light and positioned on a horizontal fixed reference surface inside the 3D printing device; 

 a mirror attached to a top surface of the build platform opposite the horizontal fixed reference surface; a detector positioned on a vertical fixed reference surface inside the 3D printing device; and a beam splitter positioned between the horizontal fixed reference surface and the top surface and comprising a forty-five degree angle with respect to the horizontal fixed reference surface, wherein the collimated light from the laser propagates through the beam splitter to the mirror, reflected light from the mirror is reflected back to the beam splitter and further reflected by the beam splitter toward the detector. 12. The 3D printing device of claim 11, wherein the detector comprises an array of detectors. 13. The 3D printing device of claim 1, further comprising: a first platform detection sensor on the first linear actuator configured to detect attachment of the build platform to the first linear actuator; and a second platform detection sensor on the second linear actuator configured to detect attachment of the build platform to the second linear actuator. 14. The 3D printing device of claim 1, further comprising: a gantry system detection sensor configured to detect a position of the build platform with respect to the printing module and the washing module. 15. The 3D printing device of claim 1, further comprising: an automatic filling system configured to automatically refill the resin tank, wherein the automatic filling system comprises a resin cartridge containing resin and a compressor. 16. The 3D printing device of claim 15, wherein the automatic filling system further comprises: a level sensor inside the resin tank and configured to detect when a resin level in the resin tank falls below a first threshold, wherein the level sensor is further configured to trigger the compressor to pump the resin from the resin cartridge into the resin tank until the resin level reaches a second threshold. 17. The 3D printing device of claim 15, wherein the automatic filling system further comprises: a pressure sensor outside the resin tank and coupled to a first hose between the compressor and the resin cartridge, wherein the resin cartridge and the resin tank are coupled via a second hose, wherein the pressure sensor is configured to measure a pressure value indicative of a resin level inside the resin tank with respect to a position of the second hose inside the resin tank. 18. The 3D printing device of claim 15, wherein the automatic filling system further comprises: an infrared sensor outside the resin tank and positioned at a desired resin level of the resin tank, wherein the infrared sensor is configured to measure an actual resin level 

of the resin inside the resin tank and to trigger the compressor to fill the resin tank with the resin from the resin cartridge until the actual resin level equals the desired resin level. 19. The 3D printing device of claim 15, wherein the compressor is further configured to form air bubbles inside the resin cartridge to mix the resin inside the resin cartridge. 20. The 3D printing device of claim 1, wherein the washing module further comprises: a porous medium inside the washing solvent tank; and a compressor configured to pump air through the porous medium to produce bubbles inside the washing solvent tank that are configured to rinse the printed object.
